title: Ryoo Thule
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Ryoo|more=Thule (disambiguation)}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Ryoo Thule |homeworld=[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] (16:11), Naboo |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark gray, dusted with gold |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children=[[Jobal Naberrie/Legends|Jobal Naberrie]]{{FactFile|80|NAB 1-4|Jobal Naberrie}} |affiliation=[[Naberrie family/Legends|House Naberrie]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Padmé did not share with us the father's name. We didn't ask. Such things are private matters on Naboo. Because of the Clone Wars we hadn't seen her in several months. She was the light of our lives, and our sorrow and grief is more than you could possibly know. Why you think you have a right to come here and question me is beyond my understanding.|Ryoo Thule, to Inquisitor Malorum|}} '''Ryoo Thule''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. She was the [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of [[Jobal Naberrie/Legends|Jobal Naberrie]], the maternal grandmother of [[Sola Naberrie/Legends|Sola Naberrie]] and [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], and the great-grandmother of [[Ryoo Naberrie/Legends|Ryoo]] and [[Pooja Naberrie/Legends|Pooja Naberrie]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. She was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] by [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Malorum]] at [[Varykino/Legends|Varykino]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] after the latter threateningly insisted to get more details about Amidala's [[Reproduction/Legends|pregnancy]] and death. ==Biography== ===Early life=== [[File:JobalNaberrieFuneral-SWCTP.png|thumb|right|Thule's daughter, Jobal Naberrie]] Ryoo Thule was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. She had a sister and was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to an unidentified man who [[Death/Legends|died]] many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the end of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. From their marriage was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] a daughter, [[Jobal Naberrie/Legends|Jobal]], who had a happy childhood and received a good education. Thule was good friends with another [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] woman named [[Winama Naberrie]]. The two secretly plotted to marry off their children, Jobal and [[Ruwee Naberrie/Legends|Ruwee]], and were the first to be surprised when they both actually fell in love. The young couple got married in [[51 BBY/Legends|51 BBY]].{{FactFile|140|FAM 1-2|Skywalker Family Tree}} Winama died in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], a year before the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]].''[[Star Wars Journal: Queen Amidala]]'' Thule's great-granddaughter, [[Ryoo Naberrie/Legends|Ryoo Naberrie]], was named after her. The name was also shared by a [[Color/Legends|blue]] and yellow [[Ryoo|flower]] that grew near the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keren/Legends|Keren]]. ===Mourning and death=== {{Quote|We just got word from the Director of Essentials, who said that Malorum forced him to reveal the whereabouts of Senator Amidala's maternal grandmother. We've been trying to contact her, but she lives in seclusion and hasn't answered our comm signals.|Captain Gregar Typho, to Ferus Olin|}} [[File:PadmeFuneral-RotS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Funeral procession of Thule's granddaughter, Padmé Amidala]] In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Thule was the overseer of the [[Padmé Amidala's funeral/Legends|funeral]] of her granddaughter [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], as was the custom on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Feeling great grief at her beloved grandchild's passing, Thule spent the next six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] mourning at [[Translucence Cove]] in [[Varykino/Legends|Varykino]]. After all these months, Thule finally decided that it was [[Time/Legends|time]] to return to [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], as she knew that Amidala would not have wanted her to live in seclusion for the rest of her life. However, Thule had been having a strange feeling ever since she woke up, but tried to comfort herself by thinking that she was only anxious to let go of her grief. It was the only possible explanation why her [[Heart/Legends|heart]] fluttered, or why Amidala felt so especially close to her that morning. Thule suddenly realized she was not alone in the retreat. A man named [[Malorum]], one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]], had come to interrogate Thule about Amidala and her mysterious pregnancy. As soon as Thule saw his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], she understood what she had been feeling all morning. She knew she was going to die. Eventually, Malorum proved her right. Her hesitation and defiance to his questions led him to deduce that Amidala's offspring had not died. The Inquisitor then proceeded to kill Thule. A short while later, [[Ferus Olin]] and [[Trever Flume]] arrived in turn at Varykino. With her dying breath, Thule begged Olin to protect her granddaughter's memory. ===Aftermath=== [[File:FerusVsMalorum.jpg|thumb|right|190px|Ferus Olin and Malorum fighting to death]] {{Quote|He suspects.''"
"''Malorum?''"
"''He can't tell anyone what he knows. You must protect…''"
"''Protect what?''"
"''For Padmé. For Padmé.|Ryoo Thule and Ferus Olin|}} Malorum's knowledge of the existence of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker]] [[Twin/Legends|twins]] died with him during a [[Uprising in Theed|lightsaber duel]] with Olin. He fell to his death inside the Theed [[Power generator/Legends|power generator]] when he tried to retrieve his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] that was tumbling down the pit. Olin had watched him struggle in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], desperately trying to hold onto his lightsaber, knowing that if he lost it he would be disgraced and would never become a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|No one is 'in charge' in our funeral rites. I was there to support our grieving family.|Ryoo Thule, to Inquisitor Malorum|}} Ryoo Thule was a small and sturdy female Human with white hair and dark gray eyes dusted with gold. She had a great sense of family and cared deeply for her relatives. While walking down to Varykino's [[Unidentified Naboo lake|lake]], she could feel the presence of her lost ones, such as her husband and her recently deceased grandchild Padmé Amidala. Thule was devastated when Amidala—to whom she had felt closer than any of her other granddaughters—mysteriously died, shortly after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The tragedy led Thule to live in isolation for six months. Thule also seemed to have a certain sense of [[Precognition/Legends|precognition]]. On the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of her death, she woke up before dawn with the unsettling feeling that something was wrong, and as soon as she saw Malorum, she knew the Inquisitor would eventually kill her. When Malorum confronted Thule, he felt that the elderly woman was full of love, strength, and courage. Even though she was deeply afraid of him and already knew what he would do to her, Thule stood up to the Inquisitor and refused to tell him what he wanted to know out of love and loyalty to Amidala. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|It is not known who Jobal Naberrie's father was, but her mother is thought to have been called Ryoo, after the blue and yellow flower that grew in the outlying hills near the village of Keren.|Jobal Naberrie's article in ''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 80|}} The character of Ryoo Thule was first mentioned in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 80|eightieth issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'', published in [[2003]]. She later made her first appearance in [[Jude Watson]]'s young reader novel ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'', released in [[April]] [[2006]].''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{FactFile|80|NAB 1-4|Jobal Naberrie}} {{1stm}} *{{FactFile|105|NAB 7-8|Sola Naberrie}} *{{InsiderCite|86|Jedi Library}} *''[[Star Wars Annual 2009]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|ryoonaberrie|Naberrie, Ryoona}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ryoo Thule |es=Ryoo Thule |pl=Ryoo Thule }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Thule, Ryoo}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:House Naberrie]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Naboo]]
